content:
In Android, we've got a weird situation where we want the host programs produced by kbuild to be linked against musl (for reasons outside my control). However, the toolchain we use is linked against glibc. This means that:

  • proc-macro crates must be linked against glibc, so that they can be loaded by the compiler
  • Other host rlib/bin crates must be linked against musl

I'm looking for a vibe check on a new env variable called HOSTRUSTTOOLCHAINLDFLAGS before we send to the list. It is intended to add a separate flag that can override HOSTLDFLAGS for proc-macro builds in order to use a different libc for the proc-macros. Preferred flag names? Different ways to inject that would be better?

cc @Alice Ryhl
What is TOOLCHAINmeant to mean within the flag name?
I think it comes from proc macros needing flags that are compatible with the toolchain, as they need to link with rustc.
Yeah, it's meant to mean "linker flags for the platform the rust compiler was linked against"
I guess an alternative is to just call it PROCMACROLDFLAGS.
Yeah. I'm not too attached to any particular name, just trying to get bike shedding out of the way with a faster cycle time

Ah, I see, yeah, makes sense.

Hmm... I guess we (or Rust) could have other things in the toolchain that we need to build (say, something like klint) that also require those "toolchain ld flags", but at the same time having PROCMACRO somewhere seems way more concrete/understandable, and one could also think different things may require different link flags anyway, eventually (so we may want different variables in any case).

I am happy either way. Either of those two names is fine, other variations too. I guess it all depends on how one looks at things (e.g. if we consider the RUST as important context, if we consider "proc macros" as host "programs", etc.). Perhaps I prefer to avoid TOOLCHAIN because it is too generic when someone reads it the first time (like when I read it quickly).
